src-gamberorossointl-alajmo.writer_profile: Marina Alajmo writes for Gambero Rosso International, the English-language arm of Italy's most authoritative food and wine publication. Her 2023 guide to Lecce draws on the Gambero Rosso guide's independent editorial criteria and direct restaurant assessment, covering the city's most significant dining destinations from its historic trattorias to its contemporary fine dining.
src-gamberorossointl-alajmo.article_title: Where to eat in Lecce, the best restaurants according to Gambero Rosso

src-roamandthrive-wieteska.writer_profile: Alex Wieteska is a travel writer and editor based in Puglia, Italy, whose blog Roam & Thrive focuses on health-conscious and culturally immersive travel across Southern Europe. Her restaurant guide to Lecce draws on firsthand dining experience across the city's historic center and quieter residential streets, with particular attention to local Salentine ingredients, traditional cooking techniques, and dining atmosphere.

src-cibotoday-interlandi.writer_profile: Valentino Interlandi is a journalist specializing in Italian food and wine culture, writing for CiboToday, a national food publication with regional correspondents across Italy. Based in the south, he focuses on the authentic stories behind small local restaurants, documenting the traditional trattorias and osterie that define Salentine dining culture for a local and national readership.
src-cibotoday-interlandi.article_title: La mappa delle migliori trattorie e osterie di Lecce

src-italyunseen-coti.writer_profile: Renata Coti is the founder of Italy Unseen, a travel and food site dedicated to off-the-beaten-path destinations across Italy. A resident of Lecce, she writes with the deep familiarity of a local food enthusiast who has spent years discovering the city's restaurants, cafés, and food culture—from neighbourhood osterie to the best pasticciotti, sharing the insider perspective that most visitor guides miss.
src-italyunseen-coti.article_title: Where And What To Eat In Lecce

src-wheresemmanow-myrick.writer_profile: Emma Myrick is the author of Where's Emma Now, a travel blog covering food, accommodation, and experiences across Southern Europe and beyond. Her Lecce guide was written following a personal visit to the city, with an emphasis on the restaurants and café spots she personally tried, focusing on traditional Pugliese food and locally loved spots.
src-wheresemmanow-myrick.article_title: Where to Eat in Lecce, Puglia

src-2night-giannini.writer_profile: Maria Teresa Giannini is a journalist covering the Lecce food and nightlife scene for 2night.it, a local events and dining publication active across Puglia and southern Italy. Her neighbourhood restaurant guide focuses on the trattorias and osterie in Lecce's residential districts beyond the historic center, writing for a local audience seeking authentic dining away from tourist routes.
src-2night-giannini.article_title: Lecce da local: le osterie di quartiere fuori dal centro storico

restaurants.name: Mezzo Quinto Osteria
restaurants.quote: Mezzo Quinto Osteria hasn't been open long, but it's already my new obsession! It's the perfect spot for a casual dinner, especially if you're looking for authentic Salento cooking without the fuss.
restaurants.article_title: Lecce da local: le osterie di quartiere fuori dal centro storico

restaurants.name: Osteria Da Angiulino
restaurants.quote: Osteria Da Angiulino is one of those Lecce restaurants I keep going back to, not because it's trendy or buzzy, but because the food is just solid. If you're after the kind of meal that feels like it's been cooked by someone's nonno in a back kitchen, this is where you want to be. The ciceri e tria was everything: crispy, comforting, and delicious. The bombette? Meltingly soft.
restaurants.article_title: La mappa delle migliori trattorie e osterie di Lecce

restaurants.name: Osteria degli Spiriti
dishes_mentioned: orecchiette al ragù bianco d'agnello e fonduta di caciocavallo
restaurants.quote: Osteria degli Spiriti leans into elegance without being too formal—it's the kind of Lecce dining spot where you dress up just a little, sip something cold and crisp, and settle in for a long, memorable meal. Start with the antipasto del mare—a stunning, ice-cold platter of raw seafood that might just ruin you for all other starters. It's fresh, bright, and sets the tone for what's to come.
restaurants.article_title: La mappa delle migliori trattorie e osterie di Lecce

restaurants.name: Trattoria Le Zie
restaurants.quote: Trattoria Le Zie is the best restaurant in Lecce for local, traditional Salentine cuisine, hands down. You'd walk right past if you didn't know what you were looking for: tucked behind an unmarked door on a quiet residential street, no frills, no fuss, just grandma-level cooking that knocks it out of the park every single time. Expect classic Lecce dishes done properly: silky fave e cicorie, ciceri e tria, stuffed zucchini flowers, and tender meatballs (made from horsemeat) in sauce that tastes like they've been simmering all day.
restaurants.article_title: La mappa delle migliori trattorie e osterie di Lecce

restaurants.name: Alle Due Corti
dishes_mentioned: cream of broad beans with chicory
orecchiette con le cime di rapa
restaurants.quote: If you are looking for a traditional trattoria, Alle Due Corti, owned by Giorgio and Marinella Grassi, is the right place, with a menu written in Salento and handmade pasta. A historic trattoria, where you can enjoy traditional dishes, mostly 'di terra': cream of broad beans with chicory, the very old 'lagane e ceci', 'orecchiette con le cime di rapa', the wonderful aubergine cucole (meatballs), lasagna ncannulata, lamb with onions.
restaurants.article_title: 16 Best Restaurants in Lecce, Italy

restaurants.name: Caffè storico Alvino
restaurants.quote: Everyone usually meets here and it's a ritual to stop at the historic Caffè Alvino, where Lecce personalities from the worlds of culture, politics and business used to meet. Families come to rediscover the goodness of home, both sweet and savoury, seated at tables overlooking the amphitheatre.
restaurants.article_title: Where And What To Eat In Lecce

restaurants.name: Alex
dishes_mentioned: tagliolini with lemon, stracciatella and raw shrimp
restaurants.signature_dish: tagliolini with lemon, stracciatella and raw shrimp
restaurants.quote: At Alex Ristorante, seafood is the undeniable star of the table. The kitchen draws from both sides of Salento—the Adriatic and the Ionian—so the catch arrives impossibly fresh, often tasting like it left the sea mere hours earlier. The oysters alone are worth the visit: briny, plump, and served simply enough to let their purity shine.
restaurants.article_title: 16 Best Restaurants in Lecce, Italy

restaurants.name: Al Verde – Cucina ricreativa
restaurants.quote: Opened August 2023 by Ciccio Mignogna and Martina Caracciolo, this intimate restaurant emphasizes quality small-producer ingredients with seasonal, regionally-inspired menus featuring both Italian and international influences.
restaurants.article_title: Lecce da local: le osterie di quartiere fuori dal centro storico

restaurants.name: aTipica-Trattoria Moderna
restaurants.quote: From the creators of 'Napul'è' pizzeria, this spacious venue blends traditional Salentian dishes with contemporary presentations and gourmet pizzas made with 78-hour fermentation, with seating for 120 covers.
restaurants.article_title: Lecce da local: le osterie di quartiere fuori dal centro storico

restaurants.name: Buonifico
restaurants.quote: In addition to the fine dining Red, the chef also runs Buonifico, a wholesome trattoria with traditional Salento dishes. The hotel definitely wanted to open itself to the city, as well as to world travellers. Therefore the restaurants, as well as the cocktail bar, and breakfast, are open to outside guests too.
restaurants.article_title: Where to eat in Lecce, the best restaurants according to Gambero Rosso

restaurants.name: Dall'Antiquario
restaurants.quote: A few steps from the church of Santa Chiara, amid period furniture and antique objects, Dall'Antiquario comes to life—a trattoria that takes its name from the passion of Gianni Gerardi, a collector and vintage enthusiast.
restaurants.article_title: La mappa delle migliori trattorie e osterie di Lecce

restaurants.name: Dedo Osteria
dishes_mentioned: potato and prawn magnums with lime mayo
restaurants.quote: Chef Edoardo Cannarozzo creates refined gourmet dishes using fresh local ingredients. Signature plates include potato and prawn 'magnums' with lime mayo and distinctive tagliolini with purple prawns, with a wine selection of the highest quality.
restaurants.article_title: Lecce da local: le osterie di quartiere fuori dal centro storico

restaurants.name: Duo Ristorante
dishes_mentioned: My sea I don't know you (lemon mousse, caper meringue, anchovies)
restaurants.signature_dish: My sea I don't know you
restaurants.quote: Located on Via Giuseppe Garibaldi, this Michelin-recommended gem offers a modern take on Apulian cuisine that's both inventive and deeply rooted in tradition. Chef Fabiano Viva brings a unique flair to the kitchen, drawing inspiration from his land, sea, and memories. His approach is evident in dishes like the 'My sea I don't know you' dessert—a lemon mousse with caper meringue and anchovies.
restaurants.article_title: 16 Best Restaurants in Lecce, Italy

restaurants.name: Er Polpetta 1987
restaurants.quote: This restaurant started in Rome, and only recently they brought the same recipes straight down here to Lecce. Generous portions of Roman classics in a cozy, lively, informal and welcoming atmosphere in front of Teatro Apollo.
restaurants.article_title: Where And What To Eat In Lecce

restaurants.name: I Latini
dishes_mentioned: burratina di bufala with confit cherry tomatoes and crispy capocollo
sagna incannulata with fresh tomato, ricotta forte and burnt leek
millefoglie di polpo with purple potatoes
restaurants.quote: Start your meal with their polpettine di melanzane, a delightful take on eggplant meatballs, or the burratina di bufala accompanied by confit cherry tomatoes and crispy capocollo. For the main course, the sagna incannulata—a traditional rolled pasta served with fresh tomato, ricotta forte, and burnt leek—is a standout. Seafood enthusiasts might opt for the millefoglie di polpo, featuring layers of octopus and purple potatoes with basil drops.
restaurants.article_title: 16 Best Restaurants in Lecce, Italy

restaurants.name: Il Vico del Gusto
restaurants.quote: When I'm in the mood for top-quality seafood in Lecce, Il Vico del Gusto is always my go-to! Fresh local seafood served in an elegant yet cozy setting with white linen tablecloths—the seafood antipasto tasting is not to be missed.
restaurants.article_title: Where And What To Eat In Lecce

restaurants.name: La Bocca
restaurants.quote: Inside the beautifully restored Palazzo Zimara, La Bocca Restaurant offers one of Lecce's more polished dining experiences. The contemporary Mediterranean restaurant focuses on seasonal, locally sourced ingredients and a thoughtful interpretation of regional cuisine, served either in an elegant dining room or in the intimate inner courtyard that feels like a secret garden in the middle of the historic center.
restaurants.article_title: 16 Best Restaurants in Lecce, Italy

restaurants.name: La Cucina di Mamma Elvira
restaurants.quote: This charming eatery offers a menu that pays homage to Salento's culinary traditions, with dishes like ciceri e tria (pasta with chickpeas) and pezzetti di cavallo (horse meat stew). The atmosphere is relaxed and welcoming, making it an ideal place to unwind after a day of exploring the city.
restaurants.article_title: 16 Best Restaurants in Lecce, Italy

restaurants.name: Osteria 203
restaurants.quote: Osteria 203 is a low-key gem that blends classic Lecce comfort food with just enough modern flair to keep things interesting. Go for the cozze gratin (mussels gratin) to start, then dive into their rich, handmade orecchiette con sugo di braciole—a true local favorite and a must-try in Puglia. Portions are generous, flavors are bold, and the vibe is effortlessly relaxed.
restaurants.article_title: 16 Best Restaurants in Lecce, Italy

restaurants.name: Osteria Da Cosimino e Figli
dishes_mentioned: purè di fave con cicorie selvatiche
foglie di ulivo pasta with porcini and wild arugula
restaurants.quote: Affectionately known by locals as 'Lu Cosiminu', this family-run osteria has been serving up traditional Salentine fare since 1960. The menu is a celebration of regional classics: start with the antipasto della casa, featuring delights like frittini misti, roasted peppers, and purè di fave con cicorie selvatiche. For mains, the handmade foglie di ulivo pasta with porcini mushrooms and wild arugula is a standout, as is the tris di arrosto—a trio of grilled meats including sausage, skewers, and pork neck.
restaurants.article_title: 16 Best Restaurants in Lecce, Italy

restaurants.name: RED Restaurant
restaurants.quote: Simone De Siato, a native of Salento, with a refined, contemporary take on the flavours 'dellu Salentu'. Simone is a fine interpreter of signature cuisine, without overdoing it, professionally confirming a personal style that is consistent with the territory. Taste remains the main character, never disappointing, dish after dish.
restaurants.article_title: Where to eat in Lecce, the best restaurants according to Gambero Rosso

restaurants.name: 63 Osteria Contemporanea
dishes_mentioned: selezione di salumi e formaggi locali
restaurants.quote: On Viale dell'Università between the city's two gates, Napoli and Rudiae, just outside the historic center, sits a contemporary osteria led by chef Anna Cotardo, where the taste of Salentine tradition meets a modern touch.
restaurants.article_title: La mappa delle migliori trattorie e osterie di Lecce

restaurants.name: Tabisca il Vico dei Tagliati
restaurants.quote: This spot is all about the art of Italian grilling in an intimate, rustic setting. Quality meat, expertly grilled—with specialties like meat skewers and pork shank with porcini, paired with a curated wine selection.
restaurants.article_title: Where And What To Eat In Lecce

restaurants.name: Trattoria Nonna Tetti
restaurants.quote: Trattoria Nonna Tetti is one of my go-to spots in Lecce when I'm craving simple, down-to-earth Salentine food that hits all the right notes. Tucked into a quiet corner of the historic center, it's a place where the vaulted stone ceilings and rustic wooden furniture make you feel like you've stepped into Nonna's home. The ciceri e tria—a local chickpea and pasta dish with crispy fried noodles—is a standout.
restaurants.article_title: 16 Best Restaurants in Lecce, Italy

restaurants.name: Trattoria San Lazzaro
restaurants.quote: The staff pamper guests with dishes refined through years of customer dialogue. The menu includes fresh sagne 'ncannulate with cod and bottarga, strozzapreti with peas and artichokes, and octopus in earthenware pots.
restaurants.article_title: Lecce da local: le osterie di quartiere fuori dal centro storico

restaurants.name: Varius Osteria Moderna
dishes_mentioned: ciceri e tria with mussels, clams and raw prawns
restaurants.quote: Chef Michele Lazoi earned Tripadvisor's 2025 Travelers' Choice Best of the Best award (top 1% globally). The kitchen reimagines tradition—ciceri e tria is transformed by combining chickpea pasta with mussels, clams, and raw prawns.
restaurants.article_title: Lecce da local: le osterie di quartiere fuori dal centro storico
